meta.title: Saludos y presentaciones
meta.description: Beginner greetings and introductions vocabulary and grammar in Spanish: basic phrases, ser/estar, and polite expressions.
meta.description_ko: 스페인어 초급 인사·자기소개 어휘와 문형: 기본 표현, ser/estar, 정중한 표현.

explanation.en: "Hola" means "hello".

questions.prompt: ¿Qué significa «Hola»? (What does "Hola" mean?)

explanation.en: "Buenos días" means "good morning".

questions.prompt: ¿Qué significa «Buenos días»? (What does "Buenos días" mean?)

explanation.en: "¿Cómo te llamas?" means "what is your name?".

questions.prompt: ¿Qué significa «¿Cómo te llamas?»? (What does "¿Cómo te llamas?" mean?)

explanation.en: "Mucho gusto" means "nice to meet you".

questions.prompt: ¿Qué significa «Mucho gusto»? (What does "Mucho gusto" mean?)

explanation.en: "Adiós" means "goodbye".

questions.prompt: ¿Qué significa «Adiós»? (What does "Adiós" mean?)

explanation.en: "Hola" means "hello".

questions.prompt: ___, me llamo Ana. (Hello, my name is Ana.)

explanation.en: "Está" (from estar) is used to ask about someone's state or condition.

questions.prompt: Buenos días, ¿cómo ___ usted? (Good morning, how are you?)

explanation.en: "Eres" (from ser) is used to ask about origin.

questions.prompt: ¿De dónde ___ tú? (Where are you from?)

explanation.en: "Conocerte" means "to meet you".

questions.prompt: Mucho gusto, igualmente. Encantado de ___. (Nice to meet you too. Pleased to meet you.)

explanation.en: "Buenas" agrees with the feminine plural noun "tardes".

questions.prompt: ___ tardes, señor García. (Good afternoon, Mr. García.)

explanation.en: "Hasta luego" means "see you later".

questions.prompt: Hasta ___, nos vemos mañana. (See you later, we'll see each other tomorrow.)

explanation.en: "Llama" is the third person singular form of "llamarse".

questions.prompt: ¿Cómo se ___ tu profesor? (What is your teacher's name?)

explanation.en: "Eres" is the informal second person singular of "ser".

questions.prompt: Soy de España, ¿y tú, de dónde ___? (I'm from Spain, and you, where are you from?)

explanation.en: "Descanses" means "rest" in this farewell expression.

questions.prompt: Buenas noches, que ___ bien. (Good night, sleep well.)

explanation.en: "Es" is the formal third person singular of "ser", used with "usted".

questions.prompt: Perdón, ¿___ usted el señor Pérez? (Excuse me, are you Mr. Pérez?)

explanation.en: "Mío" means "mine".

questions.prompt: Encantada de conocerte, el gusto es ___. (Pleased to meet you, the pleasure is mine.)

explanation.en: "Estuvo" (past of estar) asks how the day went; but for casual present tense, "está" fits better. Correct choice: "estuvo".

questions.prompt: ¿Qué tal, cómo ___ el día? (How's it going, how has your day been?)

explanation.en: "Ayudarle" means "to help you", a common formal service phrase.
explanation.ko: 「Ayudarle」는 '당신을 돕다'라는 뜻으로 흔한 격식체 서비스 표현이다.

questions.prompt: Buenas tardes, ¿en qué puedo ___? (Good afternoon, how can I help you?)

explanation.en: "Veamos" (from verse) means "see each other".

questions.prompt: Un placer conocerte, espero que nos ___ pronto de nuevo. (A pleasure to meet you, I hope we see each other again soon.)

explanation.en: "Hablar" means "to talk".

questions.prompt: ¡Hasta pronto! Fue un placer ___ contigo. (See you soon! It was a pleasure talking with you.)

explanation.en: The first person singular reflexive form is "me llamo" (not "me llama").

questions.prompt: Hola, me llama Ana. (Hello, my name is Ana.) — find the error

explanation.en: In this question, the verb "eres" comes before the subject "tú": "eres tú" (not "tú eres").
explanation.ko: 이 의문문에서는 동사 'eres'가 주어 'tú' 앞에 와야 하므로 'eres tú'가 맞다.

questions.prompt: ¿De dónde tú eres? (Where are you from?) — find the error

explanation.en: With the formal title "señor", the formal "usted" should be used, along with question marks: "¿cómo está usted?" (not "cómo está tú").
explanation.ko: 격식 있는 호칭 'señor'와 함께는 격식체 'usted'를 쓰고 물음표를 붙여야 하므로 '¿cómo está usted?'가 맞다.

questions.prompt: Buenas tardes, señor García, cómo está tú. (Good afternoon, Mr. García, how are you.) — find the error
choices: Buenas tardes, señor García, ¿cómo está usted?
Buenas tardes, señor García, cómo está tú.
Buenas tardes, señor García, cómo estás tú.
Buenas tardes, señor García, cómo está él.

explanation.en: The sentence reads: Hola, me llamo Carlos ("Hello, my name is Carlos").
explanation.ko: 문장은 'Hola, me llamo Carlos' (안녕하세요, 제 이름은 카를로스입니다)가 된다.

questions.prompt: __________ me llamo Carlos. (Hello, my name is Carlos.)

explanation.en: The sentence reads: ¿De dónde eres tú? ("Where are you from?")
explanation.ko: 문장은 '¿De dónde eres tú?' (당신은 어디 출신인가요?)가 된다.

questions.prompt: ¿De dónde __________? (Where are you from?)
